steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function can be written as
$$
f(x)=mx+b
$$
where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the value of $f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the line information
steps.content: The slope is $-3$, so $m=-3$. The point $(0,-17)$ gives the $y$-intercept, so $b=-17$.

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content: Substitute those values into the linear form: $$ f(x)=-3x-17 $$ The answer is C.
